title: Regression: Both REGISTRIES_CONFIG_PATH and CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF do not override /etc/containers/registries.conf
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I tried using a custom registries.conf and noticed podman still defaulted to `/etc/containers/registries.conf`

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. $ echo "garbage" > \~/registries.conf; REGISTRIES_CONFIG_PATH=\~/registries.conf podman pull $ANY_IMAGE 

2. Try the same with CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF

**Describe the results you received:**

`/etc/containers/registries.conf` wasn't overriden.

**Describe the results you expected:**

`/etc/containers/registries.conf` should be overriden.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Checked with @vrothberg . He can consistently reproduce it as well.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
3.1.0-rc1
```
as well as with upstream master 


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Desktop

comments.body:
Can confirm. Also affects command-line option:
```console
# echo short-name-mode="disabled" >/tmp/registries.conf
# podman --registries-conf=/tmp/registries.conf pull sdf </dev/null
Error: error getting default registries to try: short-name resolution enforced but cannot prompt without a TTY
```

If others want to tackle the issue, please feel free. My queue is long at the moment.  What we'd need to do is to inspect _all_ places where we're using `c/image/v5/types.SystemContext` and make sure that we're setting the registries conf path.
I don't mind taking this up, but only after April 9th I guess. Assigning to myself for now, if anyone else wants to take it before that, feel free.

```
$ CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF=/etc/passwd ./bin/podman pull alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
  Near line 0 (last key parsed ''): bare keys cannot contain ':'
Error: Error initializing source docker://alpine:latest: error loading registries configuration: error loading registries configuration "/etc/passwd": Near line 0 (last key parsed ''): bare keys cannot contain ':'

$ sudo CONTAINERS_REGISTRIES_CONF=/etc/passwd ./bin/podman pull alpine
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:latest...
  Near line 0 (last key parsed ''): bare keys cannot contain ':'
Error: Error initializing source docker://alpine:latest: error loading registries configuration: error loading registries configuration "/etc/passwd": Near line 0 (last key parsed ''): bare keys cannot contain ':'
```

This seems to work for me?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since I had no contradictory reports, I am closing.
Note that this will definitely be fixed with `libimage`.
